title: polygala erioptera subsp. petraea
intro: Polygala erioptera subsp. petraea, also known as the rock fingerwort, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of Polygala erioptera subsp. petraea include the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an infusion or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
